title: be going to (do)

examples: Now I'm going to show you how it works in real life.
I'm going to pour chewing and wine.

title: will / shall
examples: I'll take still water, please.
If you need a reference, If you contact Kakao link below, I will explain it carefully.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: When you go to the restaurant, when you actually use these expressions, and if you think about the situation, you can go to the US or overseas, right away.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Stephen Crashing says the effective language teacher is someone who can provide input and help make it comprehensible in a low anxiety situation.
I can use this phrase a lot in Korea while eating sundubu, 떡볶이, 김치찌개.

Oh, can I please get water?

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: He must be watching my videos a lot in studying hard because he even remembered what kind of coffee I like.

title: have to / must
examples: KELRI-SAM It has to be fun and not stressful.
I have to be careful and watch out for motorbikes.

It's not that we have to be 직접.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: If you've been studying for a long time, I think it would be great if you could buy an updated version and study it.
If you're a doctor, If you're a little difficult to work in the US, I think it would be great if you study the whole book.
Until March 1st, I think it would be great if you don't go up the price.
I think it would be great if you could read the book in the comments.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: If you've been studying for a long time, I think it would be great if you could buy an updated version and study it.
I think it would be great if you could read the book in the comments.

title: have something done
examples: But I was especially shocked when one woman told me that after studying with my materials, she got a promotion at her job.
